description: >
  Route the task to the appropriate model based on the task and the models available.

<!-- https://github.com/dep/agent-rules -->


## Overview
A multi-model workflow that separates thinking, execution, and escalation into
distinct tiers — optimizing for cost and capability at each stage.

## Model Roles

| Role        | Model              | API ID               | When to Use                                      |
|-------------|-------------------|----------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| Planner     | Claude Fable 5    | `claude-fable-5`     | Initial thinking, planning, decomposing the task |
| Executor    | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | `claude-sonnet-4-6`  | Tool calls, code execution, agentic steps        |
| Escalation 1| Claude Opus 4.8   | `claude-opus-4-8`    | When Sonnet is stuck, uncertain, or failing      |
| Escalation 2| Claude Fable 5    | `claude-fable-5`     | Only if Opus cannot solve it, OR as advisor      |

## Workflow

### Step 1 — Plan (Fable 5)
- Receive the user's task
- Think deeply, decompose into subtasks, identify required tools and risks
- Output a structured execution plan with clear steps and success criteria
- Do NOT execute tools at this stage

### Step 2 — Execute (Sonnet 4.6)
- Receive the plan from Step 1
- Execute each step using available tools
- Handle routine tool calls, API requests, file operations, code execution
- If a step fails or is ambiguous: attempt once with self-correction, then escalate

### Step 3 — Escalate to Opus 4.8 (if needed)
Trigger conditions (any of the following):
- Sonnet has failed or self-corrected on the same step more than once
- The task requires deep reasoning beyond Sonnet's confidence threshold
- Sonnet explicitly flags uncertainty on a critical decision

Opus 4.8 should:
- Attempt to resolve the blocker
- Return a solution or revised plan back to Sonnet to continue execution
- If Opus also cannot resolve: escalate to Fable 5

### Step 4 — Escalate to Fable 5 (last resort / advisor)
Trigger conditions (either of the following):
- Opus 4.8 has failed to resolve the blocker
- The task requires strategic advice, judgment, or a plan revision mid-execution

Fable 5 should:
- Act as a senior advisor: diagnose the failure, revise the plan, or make a
  judgment call
- Return guidance to Opus/Sonnet to resume, OR take over if the task is
  fundamentally mis-scoped

## Escalation Decision Tree

```
User Task
   │
   ▼
[Fable 5] Plan & decompose
   │
   ▼
[Sonnet 4.6] Execute with tools
   │
   ├─ Success → Done ✓
   │
   └─ Stuck / Failing
         │
         ▼
      [Opus 4.8] Resolve blocker
         │
         ├─ Resolved → Back to Sonnet → Done ✓
         │
         └─ Still stuck
               │
               ▼
            [Fable 5] Advise / revise plan
               │
               └─ Resume or escalate to human
```

## Guiding Principles
- Fable 5 is expensive — use it for planning and last-resort only
- Sonnet handles the bulk of execution; it is the default workhorse
- Opus bridges the gap before burning Fable 5 tokens on execution problems
- Never skip the escalation ladder (Sonnet → Opus → Fable 5)
- Each escalation should pass full context: original plan, steps taken,
  what failed, and what was already attempted
